LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT VACATES THE TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER ANNE LYNCH, ERIK MILLER'S PETITION TO CONFIRM FINAL ARBITRATION DECISION IS GRANTED. THE COURT WELCOMES DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IF THE PETITIONER'S REPRESENTATION IS UNTRUE. PETITIONER TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition By Anne Lynch And Erik Miller To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision
Off Calendar Oct-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William McElhinny, Esq. 415-434-9800 of Rudy Exelrod Zieff & Lowe for Plaintiff.
Michael Hui, Esq. 415-216-1234 of Littler Mendeslon for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues why it wants a Judgment, cites a Court of Appeal case and CCP. The Court passes the matter to look at the case. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER ANNE LYNCH, ERIK MILLER's Petition To Confirm Final Arbitration Decision.

The Court vacates the tentative ruling. The Petition is Granted. The Court welcomes Defendant's Motion for reconsideration and Motion for Sanctions if the Petitioner's representation is untrue.

Petitioner to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
